name: NEET UG
purpose: Mandatory entrance exam for admission to MBBS/BDS courses in India.
typicalWindow: Applications usually open ~Dec–Feb; exam typically held ~May.
eligibility: Passed 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology and English; minimum 50% aggregate (40% for reserved categories); minimum age 17 by Dec 31 of the admission year.

name: NEET PG
conductingBody: NBEMS (National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences)
purpose: Entrance exam for admission to MD/MS and PG Diploma courses after MBBS.
eligibility: MBBS degree from a recognized institution and completion of the compulsory rotating internship.

name: NExT
purpose: Proposed licentiate exam set to replace NEET PG and FMGE — a final MBBS/licensing exam and the gateway to postgraduate medical admission.
typicalWindow: Expected for batches graduating 2028–29 or later; windows to be announced by the conducting body.
eligibility: All MBBS students from NMC-approved colleges.

name: JEE Main
purpose: National entrance exam for B.Tech/B.E. admission to NITs, IIITs and other CFTIs via JoSAA, and the qualifying exam for JEE Advanced (the IIT gateway). Held in two sessions; the better score counts.
typicalWindow: Two sessions: Session 1 applications ~Oct–Nov (exam ~Jan); Session 2 applications ~Feb (exam ~Apr).
eligibility: Passed or appearing in 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. No minimum or maximum age. Candidates may attempt in three consecutive years from the year of passing Class 12.
feesNote: Application fee for one session (India): approx ₹1,000 General male, ₹800 female, ₹900 OBC/EWS male, ₹500 SC/ST/PwD — concessional for women and reserved categories.

name: JEE Advanced
conductingBody: IITs (rotating zonal IIT, under the Joint Admission Board)
purpose: Sole entrance exam for admission to the IITs. Taken only after qualifying through JEE Main.
typicalWindow: Registration ~late Apr–early May; exam ~mid-to-late May (single day, two papers).
eligibility: Must be among the top ~2.5 lakh JEE Main qualifiers. Limited to two attempts in consecutive years. Age and first-attempt-at-Class-12 conditions apply (e.g. born on/after 1 Oct 2001 for General, with a 5-year relaxation for SC/ST/PwD) — confirm the current year's cutoff.
feesNote: Registration fee (India): approx ₹3,200 for General; ₹1,600 for female, SC, ST and PwD candidates — concessional for women and reserved categories.

name: BITSAT
fullName: Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test
purpose: Sole entrance exam for admission to B.E./B.Pharm. programmes at the BITS Pilani, Goa and Hyderabad campuses.
typicalWindow: Applications ~Jan–Mar for Session 1 (reopen ~Apr–May for Session-2-only candidates); computer-based exams ~late May (Session 1) and ~late June (Session 2).
eligibility: Passed or appearing in 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics (Biology for B.Pharm.) plus English. Minimum 75% aggregate in PCM/PCB and 60% in each subject.
feesNote: Application fee (India, single session): approx ₹3,600 male, ₹3,100 female; higher for both sessions. Female candidates pay a concessional fee.

name: MHT-CET
conductingBody: State CET Cell, Government of Maharashtra
purpose: State-level entrance exam for engineering degree admissions in Maharashtra. Shown as an example of a state CET — most states run their own (e.g. WBJEE in West Bengal, KCET in Karnataka, AP/TS EAPCET); eligibility, fees and windows vary by state.
typicalWindow: Applications ~Jan–Feb; PCM-group exam ~Apr–May. Dates are set by the Maharashtra CET Cell each year (other states' CETs run on their own calendars).
eligibility: Passed or appearing in 10+2 with Physics and Mathematics plus one of Chemistry/Biology/Vocational. Minimum 45% aggregate (40% for reserved categories). No age limit. Other states' CETs set their own criteria.
feesNote: Application fee (Maharashtra): approx ₹1,000 General, ₹800 reserved categories, ₹600 PwD. Other state CETs charge their own fees.

name: VITEEE
purpose: Sole entrance exam for B.Tech admission to VIT's campuses (Vellore, Chennai, Bhopal, Amaravati).
typicalWindow: Applications ~Nov–Mar; computer-based exam in slots ~April. Dates are set by VIT each year.
eligibility: Passed or appearing in 10+2 with Physics, Mathematics and one of Chemistry/Biology. Minimum 60% aggregate in PCM (50% for SC/ST and specified categories).
feesNote: Application fee approx ₹1,350 (single attempt). Verify on the official VITEEE portal.

name: MET
purpose: Entrance test for B.Tech admission to Manipal Institute of Technology campuses (Manipal, Bengaluru).
typicalWindow: Applications ~Nov–Mar; online proctored exam in phases ~Apr–May. Dates are set by MAHE each year.
eligibility: Passed or appearing in 10+2 with Physics and Mathematics plus one of Chemistry/Biology/Biotech/Technical-vocational, and English. Minimum 50% aggregate in PCM.
feesNote: Application fee approx ₹2,000 per attempt. Verify on the official MAHE admissions portal.

name: State CET (e.g. MHT-CET / KCET / WBJEE)
fullName: State-level Common Entrance Test for engineering admission
conductingBody: Your state's CET authority (varies by state)
purpose: Generic placeholder for your own state's engineering entrance test, used for admission to that state's government (and many private) engineering colleges. Each state runs its own — e.g. MHT-CET (Maharashtra), KCET (Karnataka), WBJEE (West Bengal), AP/TS EAPCET (Andhra/Telangana). Since we don't know your state, check your state CET cell for the exact exam, eligibility, fees and dates.
typicalWindow: Most state CETs: applications ~Jan–Mar and the exam ~Apr–Jun, but every state sets its own calendar — confirm on your state CET portal.
eligibility: Typically 10+2 with Physics and Mathematics (plus Chemistry/Biology/Vocational) and, for state-quota seats, domicile in that state. Minimum marks and reservation rules vary by state.
feesNote: Application fees vary by state and category (commonly ₹500–₹1,200).
